codeReview.summary: The feature correctly wires createIfMissing through the native document write path and removes the brittle ProblemsView.toString assertions, but it does not fully meet the dirty-buffer reload/revert requirement. editorStore.reloadFile() still reloads from disk unconditionally, and both diff accept/reject and assistant revert call reloadFile() immediately after writing, which can still overwrite a dirty in-memory buffer. The committed Problems regression test also only checks store state and no longer verifies the layout contract it claims to guard.

issues.description: reloadFile() still calls fileService.reload() without any dirty-buffer guard in the reviewed commit, so callers can still overwrite unsaved editor content. This contradicts the feature requirement that reload/revert flows no longer overwrite dirty editor buffers without warning.

Both diff accept and reject immediately call editorStore.reloadFile(filePath) after writing. Because reloadFile() is still unconditional, these revert flows can still replace a dirty in-memory buffer from disk, so the requested hardening is not actually enforced end to end.

Assistant revert now uses expectedVersion, but it still falls back to force: expectedVersion === undefined. For a path that is open in the editor but missing from the native document cache, this still allows a blind force-write instead of preserving trustworthy conflict semantics for the first write.

The rewritten Problems test no longer checks the layout or empty-state shell it claims to guard; it only asserts store counters and filtered state. That is more durable than compiled-source string checks, but it drops direct regression coverage for the panel layout contract described by the test name and feature summary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the stability-hardening skill, but the skill's reproduction guidance is too weak for document-core reload safety: the transcript declared the dirty-buffer overwrite gap fixed even though the committed reloadFile() path remained unconditional. The procedure should explicitly require checking all callers of the hardened flow, not just targeted tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 2 says to reproduce the visible regression before fixing it, while the transcript states that revert and diff writes use version-aware conflict semantics instead of unconditional force writes even though commit 7034946 leaves editorStore.reloadFile() unconditional and diff and assistant revert still call it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Document-core shared knowledge is missing an explicit note that native document dirty state and editor dirty state must be validated together when reviewing reload/revert flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The feature targeted dirty-buffer safety, but neither AGENTS.md nor .factory/library documented the need to inspect both fileService native cache state and editorStore.reloadFile() callers; the worker transcript focused on tests and missed the remaining unconditional reload path.

summary: Reviewed feature document-core-save-reload-and-problems-followup using the feature metadata, worker transcript skeleton, skill instructions, and commit 7034946f8c09b1563d8bf8bb236a75f0022c204e. The feature improves create-if-missing wiring and replaces brittle Problems string assertions, but it does not fully satisfy the dirty-buffer reload/revert hardening contract, so the review fails.
